mnemonics: C's Main Event: Arguments, Return, Environment
keyPoints: The main function is the entry point of a C program.
Its standard signature is int main(int argc, char *argv[], char *envp[]).
It returns an integer exit status to the operating system (0 typically means success).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to include a return statement, which leads to an undefined exit status.
Incorrectly parsing command-line arguments, e.g., treating argv[0] (the program name) as the first user argument.
